summary.what: Address prime compact follow-ups: full IDs, PRIME.md hint, JSON mode, health width
summary.why: Compact output should keep agent affordances (resolvable IDs, custom-workflow visibility) and JSON should report the requested mode honestly
summary.how: Removed compactEntryID so the full tb_<ts>_<sha> ID is printed; loadWorkflowContent now returns whether PRIME.md was overridden so compact emits a hint and JSON exposes custom_workflow; runPrime sets Mode=full before the JSON branch; aligned compact health truncation to 96 chars to match entries; added focused tests for each behavior

notes: Issue 1 cost ~50 bytes per session (3 entries) but recovers paste-into-timbers-show ergonomics — the ellipsis form was unresolvable. Issue 2 favors a hint over auto-merging PRIME.md content into compact: keeps compact tight while flagging that customization exists. Issue 4 was a structural bug (Mode field hardcoded before flag interpretation) that would have leaked into MCP/JSON consumers.
